name: USS SC-739 (SC-739)
events: 26 Jul 1943 HrMs K XII (Lt.Cdr. T. Brunsting, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Fremantle with USS SC-739. (1)
17 Aug 1943 HrMs K XII (Lt.Cdr. T. Brunsting, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Fremantle with USS SC-739. (1)
19 Aug 1943 HrMs K XII (Lt.Cdr. T. Brunsting, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Fremantle with USS SC-739. (1)
29 Sep 1943 HrMs K XII (Lt.Cdr. T. Brunsting, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Fremantle with USS SC-739. (1)
19 Jan 1944 HrMs K XII (Lt.Cdr. T. Brunsting, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Fremantle with USS SC-739. (1)
22 Jan 1944 HrMs K XII (Lt.Cdr. T. Brunsting, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Fremantle with USS SC-739.
HrMs K XII was suffering from more and more defects due to her old age. Repairs were again undertaken but the boat was not used for exercises after this day. (1)

16 Jun 1944 USS Aspro (Cdr. W.A. Stevenson, USN) ended her 3rd war patrol at Fremantle, Australia. She was escorted in by USS SC-739. Refit was commenced by submarine division 122 relief crew and was completed on 30 June 1944.

29 Jul 1945 Around 1430 H/29, USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) and USS Kraken (T/Cdr. T.H. Henry, USN) departed from Fremantle for their 5th and 4th war patrols respectively. They were ordered to patrol in the South China Sea and Java Sea respectively.

On departure exercises were carried out with USS Corpus Christie (Cdr. W.W. Childress, USCG) and USS SC-739 (Lt.(jg) H. Richardson, Jr., USNR) until 2200 H/29. The submarines then set course for Lombok Strait carrying out more exercises en-route. (2)
